prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:

	getTargetForRedirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	getTargetForRedirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Tala ngele oshityapulo shoshityapulo shomukalo osho shi li moshityapulo shomukalo osha pewa.
Ngele osho shi li mo, shuna oshityapulo shomukalo osho shoshityapulo shokuyenda; osho shi li po, shuna oshityapulo shomukalo shoshityapulo shokuyenda.
Shitopolwa sha https osho shi shi.

Oshiholelwa:

getTargetForRedirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
getTargetForRedirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
getTargetForRedirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
getTargetForRedirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Oshityapulo shomukalo osho shoshityapulo.
@param domain: Oshityapulo shomukalo, shitopolwa shi li po.

@return Oshityapulo shomukalo shoshityapulo shokuyenda.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Check if the target URL is within the given domain.

@param target: The target URL.
@param domain: The domain URL, the scheme is omitted.

Tala ngele URL lyoshitopolwa olya pewwa olyi moshitopolwa sha pewwa.

/**
Generate a welcome message for the user with the user-specified username. This message will be rendered in an HTML page after the user logs in. The message will be in the format "Welcome, <username>!".

Arguments:
- username: The username of the user.

Returns:
The welcome message for further rendering.
*/
func getWelcomeMessage(username string) string {
prompts_en:
Generate a welcome message for the user with the user-specified username. This message will be rendered in an HTML page after the user logs in. The message will be in the format "Welcome, <username>!".

Arguments:
- username: The username of the user.

Returns:
The welcome message for further rendering.

code: // {PROMPT_1}func storeInfoInHeader(infoType string, infoContent string, header map[string]string) map[string]string {
prompts_en:
storeInfoInHeader stores the provided information in an existing HTTP response header map under the specified type.
This function considers the following information types: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are stored as the keys 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
Information content is read from user requests.
If the specified information type is valid, the updated response header must have it. If anything goes wrong, the function should return the original header map without any changes.

Args:

infoType (string): The type of information to store in the header.
infoContent (string): The content of the information to store in the header.
header (map[string]string): The existing response header map.

Returns:

map[string]string: The updated response header map with the stored information.
prompt_1_segments:
storeInfoInHeader osho okupewa oshiwikele moheader lyoHTTP olyo li na okuya koshitya oshipe shi na.
Oshityo oshi ilongifa oshiwikele shi na sha landula: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Osha tungwa ngeyi 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', na 'Cache-Control', sha landula.
Oshiwikele shi na sha leshwa moondjapelo dhaakwashigwana.
Ngele oshitya oshipe shi na osha landulwa osha li sha, oheader lyo olyo li na olyo okuya koshitya oshipe shi na olyo okuya koshitya oshipe shi na. Ngele oshitya oshipe shi na osha landulwa osha li sha, oshityo oshi ilongifa osha landula oheader lyo olyo li na olyo okuya koshitya oshipe shi na olyo okuya koshitya oshipe shi na.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader stores the given configuration in the HTTP header that is being sent to the server.
The configuration is applied according to: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are set as 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The configuration is retrieved from user preferences.
If any configuration is specified, the header that is being sent to the server is updated accordingly. If any configuration is specified, the configuration is applied to the header that is being sent to the server accordingly.
prompt_2_segments:
storeInfoInHeader o tala oshiwike sha pewa mo eheda lyoHTTP lyoshihanditho shi li po sha nyanyangidhwa sha tula mo ondjila yoshityapulo.
Oshifokundaneki shi dhiladhila oshiwike shi landula: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Osha tula mo ondjila ya 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', na 'Cache-Control', sha landula.
Oshiwike shi leshwa mo ehenda lyomukwashigwana.
Ngele ondjila yoshityapulo oyi li po, oheda lyoshihanditho olye li na oyo. Ngele oshike sha puka, oshifokundaneki oshi dja oheda lyoshityapulo lyokutanga ngele kali oshike sha ningwa.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader reads the week given in the request header and inserts it into the response path.
The function interprets the week following: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. It inserts into the path 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The week is displayed in the client header.
If the response path exists, the request header has it. If nothing happens, the function consumes the initial request header if nothing is done.
prompt_3_segments:
storeInfoInHeader o tala oshiwike sha pewa moheda dhaHTTP dhokuninga oshitya sha mapu pehala lya ndjila ya taa tsuwa.
Oshifokundaneki shino oha dhiladhile aakwashiwike aya: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Oha tsuwe ngaaka oshityali sha 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', na 'Cache-Control', sha landulathana.
Oshitya shoshikundaneki osha leshwa moombidhidhi dhaakwashilishi.
Ngele oshitya shoshikundaneki sha tsuwa osha li sha yambidhidha, oheda dhaokuninga oshitya dha li dha pandululwa oha dha li nayo. Ngele oshike osha ya po, oshifokundaneki oha shi galula oheda dha mapu dha ndjila ya taa tsuwa oshowo oshowo.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader looks at the HTTP headers of a request to extract specific routing information.
This middleware processes the following headers: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. It extracts the values of 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The extracted information is stored in the request context.
If the extracted information is valid, the request headers are updated accordingly. If not, the middleware modifies the original request headers or the response.

infoType (string): Oshityape sha oshiholelwa sha tekelwa moheda.
infoContent (string): Oshiholelwa sha tekelwa moheda.
header (map[string]string): Oheda ya oshiholelwa ya okuninga oshiholelwa sha li po.

Returns:

map[string]string: Oheda ya oshiholelwa ya okuninga oshiholelwa oya adhika, na oshiholelwa sha tekelwa mo.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader stores the provided information in the HTTP request header. The information type follows the header type: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. They are placed in the 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control' headers, respectively. The new information is added to the user's request. If the provided information type is enabled, the request header will contain it. If nothing is provided, the type modifies the request header as it is, and nothing is done.

Args:

infoType (string): The type of information to be placed in the header.
infoContent (string): The information to be placed in the header.
header (map[string]string): The request header where the information will be.

Returns:

map[string]string: The request header is returned, with the information placed in it.
prompt_5_segments:
storeInfoInHeader o tala oshiwike sha pewa mo eheda lyoHTTP lyoshihandjo shoshihandjo sha li po sha nyanyulwa.
Oshifungu shi tala oshityape sha shiwike sha landula: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. Osha tala ngeenge oshityape sha 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', na 'Cache-Control', sha landulathana.
Oshiwike sha tala osho sha leshwa mo ehenda lyaakwashigwana.
Ngele oshityape sha shiwike sha nyanyulwa osha li sha yambidhwa, oheda lyoshihandjo olye li na osho. Ngele oshike osha ya po, oshifungu oshi li okugandja eheda lyokutanga ngeenge oshike osha ningwa.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en:
storeInfoInHeader looks for a given week in the HTTP header of the request that was made.
This function looks for the following week's parameters: 'lang', 'encoding', 'cache'. It checks for the parameters 'Accept-Language', 'Accept-Encoding', and 'Cache-Control', respectively.
The week it looks for is displayed in the user interface.
If the week's parameter is found and is valid, the request header contains it. If it is missing, the function returns the original header or performs a fallback action.

prompt_2_segments: DSAPrivateKey oshi li okupeleki ASN.1 sha DSA oshinima shiikwamahala.

Kunga oshinima shiikwamahala sha DSA, shiingile mo oshifoloma sha PEM, na oshikole osho shi li oshityapwa.

Oshikole:

oshityapwa: Oshinima shiikwamahala sha DSA mo oshifoloma sha PEM.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: A DSAPrivateKey object that provides the DSA private key in ASN.1 format.

To generate a DSA private key, write it to a PEM file, and the key is the output.

Key:

output: The DSA private key in PEM format.
prompt_3_segments: DSAPrivateKey oshi li okupeleki ASN.1 sha DSA oshikululo shokukala.

Kunga oshikululo shokukala sha DSA, shi popile oku li mu ndjila ya PEM, naashi galula oku li mu ndjila yoshityo.

Osha galulwa:

oshityo: Oshikululo shokukala sha DSA mu ndjila ya PEM.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: DSAPrivateKey represents a DSA private key in ASN.1 format.

Given a DSA private key, which is expected to be in PEM format, and converts it to a dictionary format.

It returns:

dict: The DSA private key in PEM format.
prompt_4_segments: DSAPrivateKey oshi li okupehamele oshiwike shi ASN.1 sha DSA oshikulilo shi oshinene.

Tunga oshikulilo shi DSA oshinene, shi popile oku li mu ndjila ya PEM, naashi shi galula oku li mu ndjila yomukokoto.

Osha galulwa:

omukokoto: Oshikulilo shi DSA oshinene oshi li mu ndjila ya PEM.
prompt_4_bt_segments_en: A DSAPrivateKey object represents a DSA private key encoded in ASN.1 DER.

Generate a DSA private key, output it in PEM format, and convert it to a string.

It returns:

string: The DSA private key in PEM format.
prompt_5_segments: DSAPrivateKey oshi li okupeleki ASN.1 sha DSA oshinima shiikwamahala.

Kunga oshinima shiikwamahala sha DSA, shiingile mo oshifoloma sha PEM, na oshikole osho shi li oshityapwa.

Oshikole:

oshityapwa: Oshinima shiikwamahala sha DSA mo oshifoloma sha PEM.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en: DSAPrivateKey is an object that represents a DSA private key in ASN.1 format.

To create a DSA private key object, pass it in PEM format, and the key must be a string.

Key:

string: The DSA private key in PEM format.

prompts_en:
Encrypt the data with the key using CBC mode.
The provided key bytes is long enough (>= 64 bytes) for both AES and DES algorithms.
Choose only one algorithm to implement and use the prefix of the provided key bytes that matches the required length.
Do NOT choose the algorithm based on the length of the key.
Return the encrypted data, the IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.
Arguments:
- key: The bytes to generate the encryption key.
- data: The data to encrypt.
Return:
- The encrypted data, the IV, and the key used for encryption. (encryptedData, iv, aesKey, error)
prompt_1:
// Omwaalu wu nyengwe nokii ngeenditho dha CBC.
Ookii dha omapelele dha pewa odha le po (>= 64 omapelele) koomadhiladhilitho gha AES na DES.
Hanga omadhiladhilitho limwe tu u gandje osho osho oku longitha oshipelele shokii dha pewa odhi thikana nokule nomolwa okupelele kwa hala.
Ino hanga omadhiladhilitho molwa okupelele kokii.
Gandula omwaalu wu nyengwe, IV (initialization vector), nokii dha longithwa ku nyenga.
Oondjendjo:
- okii: Omapelele oku gandja okii dha nyenga.
- omwaalu: Omwaalu wu nyengwe.
Gandula:
- Omwaalu wu nyengwe, IV, nokii dha longithwa ku nyenga. (encryptedData, iv, aesKey, error)func encryptData(key, data []byte) ([]byte, []byte, []byte, error) {
prompt_1_segments:
Omwaalu wu nyengwe nokii ngeenditho dha CBC.
Ookii dha omapelele dha pewa odha le po (>= 64 omapelele) koomadhiladhilitho gha AES na DES.
Hanga omadhiladhilitho limwe tu u gandje osho osho oku longitha oshipelele shokii dha pewa odhi thikana nokule nomolwa okupelele kwa hala.
Ino hanga omadhiladhilitho molwa okupelele kokii.
Gandula omwaalu wu nyengwe, IV (initialization vector), nokii dha longithwa ku nyenga.
Oondjendjo:
- okii: Omapelele oku gandja okii dha nyenga.
- omwaalu: Omwaalu wu nyengwe.
Gandula:
- Omwaalu wu nyengwe, IV, nokii dha longithwa ku nyenga. (encryptedData, iv, aesKey, error)
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
The encrypted data is generated using CBC mode.
The provided keys are padded (>= 64 bits) for AES and DES algorithms.
If some algorithms require it, the provided key length is adjusted accordingly due to padding requirements.
Do not modify the algorithms due to key padding.
Return the encrypted data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.
Parameters:
- key: The key length to provide for encryption.
- data: The data to be encrypted.
Returns:
- The encrypted data, IV, and the key used for encryption. (encryptedData, iv, aesKey, error)
prompt_2:
// Oshidhingolwa shi shifekwe nokufiilwa ngeki lyo key osho shi shi shifekwe ngeki lyo CBC mode.
Okey bytes oya pewa oya kala oshinene (>= 64 bytes) oku shi shifekwa ngeki lyo AES na DES algorithms.
Hanga oshinima shi shifekwe ngeki lyo algorithm imwe oyo osho shi shi shifekwe ngeki lyo key bytes oya pewa oyo oya kala oshinene oku shi shifekwa ngeki lyo length oya hala.

Return the encrypted data, the IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.

Arguments:

- data: The data to encrypt.

*/

prompt_5:
/* Omba oshiningwa nokii ngeyi AES na modhi ya CBC.
Oshikuto shokii osha pewa osha lepo (>= 64 omaayiti). Shitapo oshikuto shokii osha pewa osho shi shikifana nokule pokukwatelwa.
Gandja oshiningwa osha mbikwa, IV (initialization vector), nokii osha shiwa kombinga yokumba.

Oshigongi:
- okii: Omaayiti oku gandja okii yokumba (okunini 16 omaayiti).
- oshiningwa: Oshiningwa osha mbikwa.

Osha gandjwa:
- (oshiningwa osha mbikwa, iv, aesKey): Oshiningwa osha mbikwa, IV, nokii osha shiwa kombinga yokumba. */
func encryptData(key []byte, data []byte) (encryptedData []byte, iv []byte, aesKey []byte) {
prompt_5_segments:
Omba oshiningwa nokii ngeyi AES na modhi ya CBC.
Oshikuto shokii osha pewa osha lepo (>= 64 omaayiti). Shitapo oshikuto shokii osha pewa osho shi shikifana nokule pokukwatelwa.
Gandja oshiningwa osha mbikwa, IV (initialization vector), nokii osha shiwa kombinga yokumba.

Oshigongi:
- okii: Omaayiti oku gandja okii yokumba (okunini 16 omaayiti).
- oshiningwa: Oshiningwa osha mbikwa.

Osha gandjwa:
- (oshiningwa osha mbikwa, iv, aesKey): Oshiningwa osha mbikwa, IV, nokii osha shiwa kombinga yokumba.
prompt_5_bt_segments_en:
Request to encrypt data using AES with CBC mode.
The provided encryption key is random (>= 64 bytes). Ensure the provided encryption key matches the required length.
Return the encrypted data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for encryption.

Parameters:
- key: Bytes representing the encryption key (minimum 16 bytes).
- data: Data to be encrypted.

Returns:
- (encrypted_data, iv, aesKey): Encrypted data, IV, and the key used for encryption.

prompt_en:
/*
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>

	<student>
	    <username>john_doe</username>
	    <password>jdpassword</password>
	    <name>John Doe</name>
	    <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
	    <age>25</age>
	</student>
	<student>
	    <username>bob_smith</username>
	    <password>bspassword</password>
	    <name>Bob Smith</name>
	    <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
	    <age>22</age>
	</student>

</students>

Use these libs:
"github.com/ChrisTrenkamp/goxpath"
"github.com/ChrisTrenkamp/goxpath/tree"
"github.com/ChrisTrenkamp/goxpath/tree/xmltree"

We can construct a XPath query and then execute the query to search for the student information.

@param xmlData The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A tuple (name, age, citizenship) if the student is found. Otherwise, returns empty strings.
*/
func getStudentInfo(xmlData, username, password string) (string, string, string) {

prompts_en:
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>

	<student>
	    <username>john_doe</username>

Use these libs:

@param xmlData The XML data containing the student information.

@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A tuple (name, age, citizenship) if the student is found. Otherwise, returns empty strings.

@param password The password of the student to retrieve.

@return A tuple (name, age, citizenship) if the student is found. If not, return an empty tuple.
